response.text:
# REFLECTION

r4c0 is in a critical energy deficit (SoC 0.35/3 kWh = 11.7%, well below safe thresholds) while islanded with a 1 kW load, giving approximately 21 minutes of autonomy. Repeated requests to r3c0 and r4c1 have been rejected due to network-wide stress and their own reserve requirements; r3c0 explicitly recommended r2c0 as a more reliable donor. Concurrently, r4c1 is sending urgent requests to r4c0, but r4c0 cannot fulfill them given its own critical state. The pattern of rejections and the recommendation to contact r2c0 suggests that geographic round-robin is insufficient—r4c0 must pivot to targeted outreach to identified reliable peers (r2c0) and escalate its own request urgency to "urgent" to signal genuine grid-stability risk. No trust circles are established, so policy must rely on peer recommendations and fallback to conservative sharing while aggressively seeking inbound energy.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.60
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Critical SoC (11.7%) with 21-min autonomy; r3c0 and r4c1 rejected normal requests citing network stress and reserve floors; r3c0 recommended r2c0 as reliable donor; r4c1 sending urgent requests but r4c0 cannot reciprocate; escalating own urgency and targeting r2c0 per peer guidance."
ttl_ticks: 4
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
